evidence: Brazil's opening draw made the Haiti match more important for restoring momentum and goal difference. The later 3-0 win directly answered the pressure created by the draw.
When a favorite drops points early, other group teams gain a plausible path to challenge for the top spots. Brazil's draw made Group C less predictable.
Brazil's recovery win kept them in position to control the group, making the Scotland match a direct test of whether the favorite had fully stabilized.
Argentina's first clean win reduced pressure and allowed a controlled second performance. The Austria win built on the stability created against Algeria.
Two clean wins in two group matches strongly shift Argentina's interpretation from merely winning to controlling the tournament environment.
Portugal's opening draw raised the stakes of the following Uzbekistan and Colombia matches because they could no longer rely on a perfect start.
Colombia's win mattered beyond its own points because it created a direct table contrast with Portugal's draw. This increased Portugal's pressure before the next match.
A 7-1 result creates both a statistical edge and a narrative signal. Germany's win became an early benchmark for title-contender form.
Canada's heavy win changed the team's perceived ceiling and goal-difference position, both of which are common triggers for dark-horse narratives.
Japan's dominant 4-0 win strengthened their tournament narrative and made future opponents more likely to treat them as a knockout-stage threat.
